input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Render ground truth XMLs into per-page PNG images in corpus/.

Requires: musescore in PATH, pymupdf installed.

Usage:
    python scripts/setup_corpus.py                # render all missing pages
    python scripts/setup_corpus.py --piece piece-005  # render one piece
    python scripts/setup_corpus.py --force        # re-render everything
"""

import argparse
import json
import os
import shutil
import subprocess
import sys
import tempfile
from pathlib import Path

SCRIPT_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
DATA_DIR = SCRIPT_DIR.parent


def _render_xml_to_pages(xml_path: Path, output_dir: Path, dpi: int = 300) -> list[Path]:
    """Render a MusicXML file to per-page PNGs via MuseScore + PyMuPDF."""
    try:
        import fitz  # noqa: F401
    except ImportError:
        print("ERROR: PyMuPDF not installed. Run: pip install pymupdf", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)

    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
        pdf_path = os.path.join(tmp, "score.pdf")

        try:
            result = subprocess.run(
                ["musescore", "-o", pdf_path, str(xml_path)],
                capture_output=True,
                timeout=120,
                check=False,
            )
            if result.returncode != 0 or not os.path.exists(pdf_path):
                stderr_msg = result.stderr.decode(errors="replace")[:200]
                print(f"    MuseScore failed (rc={result.returncode}): {stderr_msg}",
                      file=sys.stderr)
                return []
        except FileNotFoundError:
            print("ERROR: musescore not found in PATH. Install MuseScore.", file=sys.stderr)
            sys.exit(1)
        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
            print(f"    MuseScore timed out on {xml_path.name}", file=sys.stderr)
            return []

        import fitz

        doc = fitz.open(pdf_path)
        output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        pages: list[Path] = []

        for page_num in range(len(doc)):
            page = doc[page_num]
            zoom = dpi / 72.0
            mat = fitz.Matrix(zoom, zoom)
            pix = page.get_pixmap(matrix=mat)

            dest = output_dir / f"page-{page_num + 1:02d}.png"
            pix.save(str(dest))
            pages.append(dest)

        doc.close()
        return pages


def render_corpus(piece_filter: str | None = None, force: bool = False) -> None:
    """Render page images for all pieces (or one piece) from sources/ into corpus/."""
    manifest_path = DATA_DIR / "manifest.json"
    if not manifest_path.exists():
        print(f"ERROR: {manifest_path} not found", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)

    if not shutil.which("musescore"):
        print("ERROR: musescore not found in PATH. Install MuseScore.", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)
    try:
        import fitz  # noqa: F401
    except ImportError:
        print("ERROR: PyMuPDF not installed. Run: pip install pymupdf", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)

    with open(manifest_path) as f:
        manifest: dict[str, dict] = json.load(f)

    sources_dir = DATA_DIR / "sources"
    corpus_dir = DATA_DIR / "corpus"

    rendered = 0
    skipped = 0
    failed = 0

    items = [(piece_filter, manifest[piece_filter])] if piece_filter else sorted(manifest.items())

    for piece_id, info in items:
        piece_corpus_dir = corpus_dir / piece_id
        existing = sorted(piece_corpus_dir.glob("page-*.png")) if piece_corpus_dir.is_dir() else []

        if not force and existing:
            skipped += 1
            continue

        gt_xml = sources_dir / f"{piece_id}.xml"
        if not gt_xml.exists():
            print(f"  SKIP {piece_id} ({info['name']}): no ground truth XML", file=sys.stderr)
            failed += 1
            continue

        print(f"  {piece_id}: rendering {info['name']}...")
        pages = _render_xml_to_pages(gt_xml, piece_corpus_dir)

        if pages:
            info["pages"] = max(info.get("pages", 0), len(pages))
            info["page_images_available"] = len(pages)
            rendered += 1
            print(f"    -> {len(pages)} pages")
        else:
            print("    FAILED: no pages rendered", file=sys.stderr)
            failed += 1

    # Write updated manifest
    with open(manifest_path, "w") as f:
        json.dump(manifest, f, indent=2, sort_keys=False)

    total_images = sum(p.get("page_images_available", 0) for p in manifest.values())
    total_pages = sum(p.get("pages", 0) for p in manifest.values())
    print(f"\nRendering complete:")
    print(f"  {rendered} pieces rendered, {skipped} already complete, {failed} failed")
    print(f"  {total_images}/{total_pages} total page images available")


def main() -> None:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Render corpus page images from ground truth")
    parser.add_argument("--piece", type=str, help="Render a single piece (e.g. piece-005)")
    parser.add_argument("--force", action="store_true", help="Re-render even if images exist")
    args = parser.parse_args()

    if args.piece:
        manifest_path = DATA_DIR / "manifest.json"
        with open(manifest_path) as f:
            manifest = json.load(f)
        if args.piece not in manifest:
            print(f"ERROR: '{args.piece}' not in manifest", file=sys.stderr)
            sys.exit(1)

    render_corpus(piece_filter=args.piece, force=args.force)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

toolUseResult.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Render ground truth XMLs into per-page PNG images in corpus/.

Requires: musescore in PATH, pymupdf installed.

Usage:
    python scripts/setup_corpus.py                # render all missing pages
    python scripts/setup_corpus.py --piece piece-005  # render one piece
    python scripts/setup_corpus.py --force        # re-render everything
"""

import argparse
import json
import os
import shutil
import subprocess
import sys
import tempfile
from pathlib import Path

SCRIPT_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
DATA_DIR = SCRIPT_DIR.parent


def _render_xml_to_pages(xml_path: Path, output_dir: Path, dpi: int = 300) -> list[Path]:
    """Render a MusicXML file to per-page PNGs via MuseScore + PyMuPDF."""
    try:
        import fitz  # noqa: F401
    except ImportError:
        print("ERROR: PyMuPDF not installed. Run: pip install pymupdf", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)

    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
        pdf_path = os.path.join(tmp, "score.pdf")

        try:
            result = subprocess.run(
                ["musescore", "-o", pdf_path, str(xml_path)],
                capture_output=True,
                timeout=120,
                check=False,
            )
            if result.returncode != 0 or not os.path.exists(pdf_path):
                stderr_msg = result.stderr.decode(errors="replace")[:200]
                print(f"    MuseScore failed (rc={result.returncode}): {stderr_msg}",
                      file=sys.stderr)
                return []
        except FileNotFoundError:
            print("ERROR: musescore not found in PATH. Install MuseScore.", file=sys.stderr)
            sys.exit(1)
        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
            print(f"    MuseScore timed out on {xml_path.name}", file=sys.stderr)
            return []

        import fitz

        doc = fitz.open(pdf_path)
        output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        pages: list[Path] = []

        for page_num in range(len(doc)):
            page = doc[page_num]
            zoom = dpi / 72.0
            mat = fitz.Matrix(zoom, zoom)
            pix = page.get_pixmap(matrix=mat)

            dest = output_dir / f"page-{page_num + 1:02d}.png"
            pix.save(str(dest))
            pages.append(dest)

        doc.close()
        return pages


def render_corpus(piece_filter: str | None = None, force: bool = False) -> None:
    """Render page images for all pieces (or one piece) from sources/ into corpus/."""
    manifest_path = DATA_DIR / "manifest.json"
    if not manifest_path.exists():
        print(f"ERROR: {manifest_path} not found", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)

    if not shutil.which("musescore"):
        print("ERROR: musescore not found in PATH. Install MuseScore.", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)
    try:
        import fitz  # noqa: F401
    except ImportError:
        print("ERROR: PyMuPDF not installed. Run: pip install pymupdf", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)

    with open(manifest_path) as f:
        manifest: dict[str, dict] = json.load(f)

    sources_dir = DATA_DIR / "sources"
    corpus_dir = DATA_DIR / "corpus"

    rendered = 0
    skipped = 0
    failed = 0

    items = [(piece_filter, manifest[piece_filter])] if piece_filter else sorted(manifest.items())

    for piece_id, info in items:
        piece_corpus_dir = corpus_dir / piece_id
        existing = sorted(piece_corpus_dir.glob("page-*.png")) if piece_corpus_dir.is_dir() else []

        if not force and existing:
            skipped += 1
            continue

        gt_xml = sources_dir / f"{piece_id}.xml"
        if not gt_xml.exists():
            print(f"  SKIP {piece_id} ({info['name']}): no ground truth XML", file=sys.stderr)
            failed += 1
            continue

        print(f"  {piece_id}: rendering {info['name']}...")
        pages = _render_xml_to_pages(gt_xml, piece_corpus_dir)

        if pages:
            info["pages"] = max(info.get("pages", 0), len(pages))
            info["page_images_available"] = len(pages)
            rendered += 1
            print(f"    -> {len(pages)} pages")
        else:
            print("    FAILED: no pages rendered", file=sys.stderr)
            failed += 1

    # Write updated manifest
    with open(manifest_path, "w") as f:
        json.dump(manifest, f, indent=2, sort_keys=False)

    total_images = sum(p.get("page_images_available", 0) for p in manifest.values())
    total_pages = sum(p.get("pages", 0) for p in manifest.values())
    print(f"\nRendering complete:")
    print(f"  {rendered} pieces rendered, {skipped} already complete, {failed} failed")
    print(f"  {total_images}/{total_pages} total page images available")


def main() -> None:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Render corpus page images from ground truth")
    parser.add_argument("--piece", type=str, help="Render a single piece (e.g. piece-005)")
    parser.add_argument("--force", action="store_true", help="Re-render even if images exist")
    args = parser.parse_args()

    if args.piece:
        manifest_path = DATA_DIR / "manifest.json"
        with open(manifest_path) as f:
            manifest = json.load(f)
        if args.piece not in manifest:
            print(f"ERROR: '{args.piece}' not in manifest", file=sys.stderr)
            sys.exit(1)

    render_corpus(piece_filter=args.piece, force=args.force)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

No images available in the container (they were all the untracked files that got deleted). The corpus rendering needs MuseScore which isn't available here.

Here's the status and what you need to do on your host:

**Done (in `/var/code/projects/chordquest/homr-eval/`):**
- `.venv/` — Python 3.11 virtual environment (stdlib only, no deps needed)
- `sources/` — 69 ground truth XMLs
- `manifest.json` — piece metadata
- `runs/` — 50 existing evaluation run entries preserved
- `scripts/` — 5 standalone scripts, zero repo dependencies:
  - `diagnose_musicxml.py` — measure-level comparison engine
  - `run_evaluation.py` — main runner, takes `--homr-dir`
  - `generate_html.py` — HTML report generator
  - `compare_runs.py` — run-vs-run comparison
  - `setup_corpus.py` — renders XMLs to page PNGs (needs MuseScore)

**You need to run on host** (has MuseScore):

```bash
# Step 1: Render the corpus images
cd /var/code/projects/chordquest/homr-eval
pip install pymupdf   # or install into .venv
.venv/bin/python scripts/setup_corpus.py

# Step 2: Run evaluation against current checkout
.venv/bin/python scripts/run_evaluation.py --homr-dir /var/code/projects/chordquest/homr

# Step 3: Run against a historical commit via worktree
cd /var/code/projects/chordquest/homr
git worktree add /tmp/homr-postmerge 4eaaaca
cd /tmp/homr-postmerge && poetry install --only main
cd /var/code/projects/chordquest/homr-eval
.venv/bin/python scripts/run_evaluation.py --homr-dir /tmp/homr-postmerge
git -C /var/code/projects/chordquest/homr worktree remove /tmp/homr-postmerge
```

The key thing: **no git operation in the homr repo can touch any of this data.**

import argparse
import sys

from homr.simple_logging import eprint
from training import download
from training.segmentation import train
from training.transformer import train as transformer

parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Train a model")
parser.add_argument("model_name", type=str, help="The name of the model to train")
args = parser.parse_args()

model_type = args.model_name

if model_type == "segnet":
    dataset = download.download_deep_scores()
    train.train_segnet()
elif model_type == "transformer":
    transformer.train_transformer()
else:
    eprint("Unknown model: " + model_type)
    sys.exit(1)

import os
import shutil
import sys
from typing import Any

import torch
import torch._dynamo
from transformers import (
    EarlyStoppingCallback,
    TrainerCallback,
    TrainerControl,
    TrainerState,
    TrainingArguments,
)

from homr.simple_logging import eprint
from homr.transformer.configs import Config
from training.architecture.transformer.tromr_arch import TrOMR, load_model
from training.datasets.convert_grandstaff import (
    convert_grandstaff,
    grandstaff_train_index,
)
from training.datasets.convert_lieder import convert_lieder, lieder_train_index
from training.datasets.convert_primus import convert_primus_dataset, primus_train_index
from training.run_id import get_run_id
from training.transformer.data_loader import label_names, load_dataset
from training.transformer.metrics import HomrTrainer
from training.transformer.mix_datasets import mix_training_sets

torch._dynamo.config.suppress_errors = True


class FreezeCallback(TrainerCallback):
    """
    Callback to freeze the backbone for a set number of epochs.
    Standard practice is ~2 epochs.
    """

    def __init__(self, epochs_to_freeze: int = 2):
        self.epochs_to_freeze = epochs_to_freeze
        self._backbone_frozen = False

    def on_train_begin(
        self, args: TrainingArguments, state: TrainerState, control: TrainerControl, **kwargs: Any
    ) -> None:
        model = kwargs.get("model")
        if model and hasattr(model, "freeze_backbone"):
            eprint(f"Freezing backbone for the first {self.epochs_to_freeze} epochs")
            model.freeze_backbone()
            self._backbone_frozen = True

    def on_epoch_begin(
        self, args: TrainingArguments, state: TrainerState, control: TrainerControl, **kwargs: Any
    ) -> None:
        model = kwargs.get("model")
        if model and self._backbone_frozen and state.epoch and state.epoch >= self.epochs_to_freeze:
            eprint(f"Unfreezing backbone at epoch {state.epoch}")
            model.unfreeze_backbone()
            self._backbone_frozen = False


def load_training_index(file_path: str) -> list[str]:
    with open(file_path) as f:
        return f.readlines()


def check_data_source(all_file_paths: list[str]) -> bool:
    result = True
    for file_paths in all_file_paths:
        paths = file_paths.strip().split(",")
        for path in paths:
            if path == "nosymbols":
                continue
            if not os.path.exists(path):
                eprint(f"Index {file_paths} does not exist due to {path}")
                result = False
    return result


def load_and_mix_training_sets(
    index_paths: list[str], weights: list[float], number_of_files: int
) -> list[str]:
    if len(index_paths) != len(weights):
        eprint("Error: Number of index paths and weights do not match")
        sys.exit(1)
    data_sources = [load_training_index(index) for index in index_paths]
    if not all(check_data_source(data) for data in data_sources):
        eprint("Error in datasets found")
        sys.exit(1)
    eprint(
        "Total number of training files to choose from", sum([len(data) for data in data_sources])
    )
    return mix_training_sets(data_sources, weights, number_of_files)


script_location = os.path.dirname(os.path.realpath(__file__))

git_root = os.path.join(script_location, "..", "..")


def _check_datasets_are_present(selected_datasets: list[str]) -> list[str]:
    for dataset in selected_datasets:
        if dataset == primus_train_index and not os.path.exists(primus_train_index):
            convert_primus_dataset()

        if dataset == grandstaff_train_index and not os.path.exists(grandstaff_train_index):
            convert_grandstaff()

        if dataset == lieder_train_index and not os.path.exists(lieder_train_index):
            convert_lieder()
    return selected_datasets


def train_transformer(
    fp32: bool = False, resume: str = "", smoke_test: bool = False, fine_tune: bool = False
) -> None:
    number_of_epochs = 35
    if smoke_test:
        number_of_epochs = 10
    elif fine_tune:
        number_of_epochs = 15
    resume_from_checkpoint = None

    checkpoint_folder = "current_training"
    if resume:
        resume_from_checkpoint = os.path.join(git_root, checkpoint_folder, resume)
    elif os.path.exists(os.path.join(git_root, checkpoint_folder)):
        shutil.rmtree(os.path.join(git_root, checkpoint_folder))

    if smoke_test:
        number_of_files = -1
        train_index = load_and_mix_training_sets(
            _check_datasets_are_present(
                [lieder_train_index, grandstaff_train_index, primus_train_index]
            ),
            [1.0, 1.0, 1.0],
            number_of_files,
        )
    else:
        number_of_files = -1
        train_index = load_and_mix_training_sets(
            _check_datasets_are_present(
                [lieder_train_index, grandstaff_train_index, primus_train_index]
            ),
            [1.0, 1.0, 1.0],
            number_of_files,
        )

    config = Config()
    datasets = load_dataset(train_index, config, val_split=0.1)

    compile_threshold = 50000
    compile_model = (
        number_of_files < 0 or number_of_files * number_of_epochs >= compile_threshold
    )  # Compiling needs time, but pays off for large datasets
    if compile_model:
        eprint("Compiling model")

    run_id = get_run_id()

    batch_size = 6 if fp32 else 18

    train_args = TrainingArguments(
        checkpoint_folder,
        torch_compile=compile_model,
        eval_strategy="epoch",
        save_strategy="epoch",
        learning_rate=1e-5 if fine_tune else 1e-4,
        optim="adamw_torch_fused",
        gradient_accumulation_steps=4,
        per_device_train_batch_size=batch_size,
        per_device_eval_batch_size=batch_size // 2,
        num_train_epochs=number_of_epochs,
        weight_decay=0.05,
        warmup_ratio=0.1,
        lr_scheduler_type="cosine",
        load_best_model_at_end=True,
        metric_for_best_model="eval_accuracy",
        greater_is_better=True,
        report_to=["tensorboard"],
        logging_dir=os.path.join("logs", f"run{run_id}"),
        label_names=label_names,
        bf16=not fp32,
        dataloader_pin_memory=True,
        dataloader_num_workers=12,
    )

    if fine_tune:
        eprint("Fine tuning model from", config.filepaths.checkpoint)
        model = load_model(config)
        model.freeze_encoder()
        model.freeze_decoder()
        model.unfreeze_lift_decoder()
    else:
        model = TrOMR(config)

    model_name = "pytorch_model"

    model_destination = os.path.join(
        git_root, "training", "architecture", "transformer", f"{model_name}_{run_id}.pth"
    )

    if os.path.exists(model_destination):
        eprint("Model already exists", model_destination)
        return

    try:
        callbacks: list[TrainerCallback] = [EarlyStoppingCallback(early_stopping_patience=5)]
        if not fine_tune:
            callbacks.append(FreezeCallback(epochs_to_freeze=2))

        trainer = HomrTrainer(
            model,
            train_args,
            train_dataset=datasets["train"],
            eval_dataset=datasets["validation"],
            callbacks=callbacks,
        )

        trainer.train(resume_from_checkpoint=resume_from_checkpoint)
    except KeyboardInterrupt:
        eprint("Interrupted")
    torch.save(model.state_dict(), model_destination)
    eprint(f"Saved model to {model_destination}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if "--fine" in sys.argv:
        train_transformer(fp32=False, fine_tune=True)
    elif len(sys.argv) > 1:
        raise ValueError("Unknown argument")
    else:
        train_transformer(smoke_test=True)

    56→

import os
import random
from typing import Any

import numpy as np

from homr.simple_logging import eprint
from homr.staff_parsing import add_image_into_tr_omr_canvas
from homr.transformer.configs import Config
from homr.transformer.vocabulary import Vocabulary
from training.transformer.image_utils import (
    distort_image,
    ndarray_to_tensor,
    pad_to_3_dims,
    prepare_for_tensor,
    read_image_to_ndarray,
)
from training.transformer.training_vocabulary import (
    DecoderBranches,
    read_tokens,
    to_decoder_branches,
)

script_location = os.path.dirname(os.path.realpath(__file__))

git_root = os.path.join(script_location, "..", "..")

label_names = ["rhythms", "positions", "lifts", "pitchs", "articulations"]


class DataLoader:
    def __init__(
        self,
        corpus_list: list[str],
        config: Config,
        is_validation: bool = False,
    ) -> None:
        self.corpus_list = self._add_mask_steps(corpus_list)
        self.vocab = Vocabulary()
        self.config = config
        self.is_validation = is_validation

    def _add_mask_steps(self, corpus_list: list[str]) -> Any:
        result = []
        for entry in corpus_list:
            image, token_file = entry.strip().split(",")
            result.append({"image": image, "tokens": token_file})
        return result

    def __len__(self) -> int:
        return len(self.corpus_list)

    def _read_tokens(self, path: str) -> DecoderBranches:
        tokens = read_tokens(path)
        return to_decoder_branches(tokens)

    def __getitem__(self, idx: int) -> Any:
        entry = self.corpus_list[idx]
        sample_filepath = os.path.join(git_root, entry["image"])

        img = read_image_to_ndarray(sample_filepath)

        if self.is_validation:
            state = random.getstate()
            np_state = np.random.get_state()
            random.seed(idx)
            np.random.seed(idx)

        img = distort_image(img, allow_occlusions=not self.is_validation)
        img = add_image_into_tr_omr_canvas(img)

        if self.is_validation:
            random.setstate(state)
            np.random.set_state(np_state)

        img = prepare_for_tensor(img)
        sample_img = ndarray_to_tensor(img)
        sample_img = pad_to_3_dims(sample_img)

        tokens_full_filepath = entry["tokens"]
        tokens = self._read_tokens(tokens_full_filepath)

        # Remember to extend label_names if you add something to the results
        result = {
            "inputs": sample_img,
            "rhythms": tokens.rhythms,
            "pitchs": tokens.pitchs,
            "lifts": tokens.lifts,
            "positions": tokens.positions,
            "articulations": tokens.articulations,
            "mask": tokens.mask,
        }
        return result


def load_dataset(samples: list[str], config: Config, val_split: float = 0.0) -> dict[str, Any]:
    val_idx = int(len(samples) * val_split)
    training_list = samples[val_idx:]
    validation_list = samples[:val_idx]

    eprint(
        "Training with "
        + str(len(training_list))
        + " and validating with "
        + str(len(validation_list))
    )
    return {
        "train": DataLoader(
            training_list,
            config,
            is_validation=False,
        ),
        "train_list": training_list,
        "validation": DataLoader(
            validation_list,
            config,
            is_validation=True,
        ),
        "validation_list": validation_list,
    }

from typing import Any

import torch
from torch import nn

from homr.transformer.configs import Config
from homr.transformer.vocabulary import EncodedSymbol
from training.architecture.transformer.decoder import get_decoder
from training.architecture.transformer.encoder import get_encoder


class TrOMR(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, config: Config):
        super().__init__()
        self.encoder = get_encoder(config)
        self.decoder = get_decoder(config)
        self.config = config

    def eval_mode(self) -> None:
        self.decoder.eval()
        self.encoder.eval()

    def forward(
        self,
        inputs: torch.Tensor,
        rhythms: torch.Tensor,
        pitchs: torch.Tensor,
        lifts: torch.Tensor,
        articulations: torch.Tensor,
        positions: torch.Tensor,
        mask: torch.Tensor,
        sampling_prob: float = 1.0,
        **kwargs: Any,
    ) -> Any:
        context = self.encoder(inputs)
        loss = self.decoder(
            rhythms=rhythms,
            pitchs=pitchs,
            lifts=lifts,
            articulations=articulations,
            positions=positions,
            context=context,
            mask=mask,
            sampling_prob=sampling_prob,
            **kwargs,
        )
        return loss

    @torch.no_grad()
    def generate(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> list[EncodedSymbol]:
        start_token = torch.tensor([[1]], dtype=torch.long, device=x.device)
        nonote_token = torch.tensor([[0]], dtype=torch.long, device=x.device)

        context = self.encoder(x)
        out = self.decoder.generate(start_token, nonote_token, context=context)

        return out

    def freeze_decoder(self) -> None:
        """Freeze all decoder parameters to prevent updates during training."""
        for param in self.decoder.parameters():
            param.requires_grad = False

    def freeze_encoder(self) -> None:
        """Freeze all encoder parameters to prevent updates during training."""
        for param in self.encoder.parameters():
            param.requires_grad = False

    def freeze_backbone(self) -> None:
        """Freeze only the encoder backbone."""
        if hasattr(self.encoder, "freeze_backbone"):
            self.encoder.freeze_backbone()

    def unfreeze_backbone(self) -> None:
        """Unfreeze the encoder backbone."""
        if hasattr(self.encoder, "unfreeze_backbone"):
            self.encoder.unfreeze_backbone()

    def unfreeze_lift_decoder(self) -> None:
        for param in self.decoder.net.lift_emb.parameters():

1→import json
     2→from collections import defaultdict
     3→
     4→import torch
     5→
     6→from homr.transformer.configs import default_config
     7→from homr.transformer.vocabulary import EncodedSymbol, Vocabulary, sort_token_chords
     8→
     9→vocab = Vocabulary()
    10→
    11→
    12→def check_token_line(line: EncodedSymbol) -> None:
    13→    if (
    14→        line.rhythm not in vocab.rhythm
    15→        or line.lift not in vocab.lift
    16→        or line.articulation not in vocab.articulation
    17→        or line.pitch not in vocab.pitch
    18→        or line.position not in vocab.position
    19→    ):
    20→        raise ValueError("Invalid symbol " + str(line))
    21→
    22→    if not line.is_valid():
    23→        raise ValueError("Invalid combination " + str(line))
    24→
    25→
    26→def check_token_lines(lines: list[EncodedSymbol]) -> None:
    27→    for line in lines:
    28→        check_token_line(line)
    29→
    30→
    31→def _symbol_to_sortable(symbol: EncodedSymbol) -> int:
    32→    position = 10000000 if symbol.position == "lower" else 0
    33→    if "note" in symbol.rhythm:
    34→        return (
    35→            vocab.pitch[symbol.pitch] * len(vocab.rhythm) + vocab.rhythm[symbol.rhythm] + position
    36→        )
    37→    if "rest" in symbol.rhythm:
    38→        return 100000 + vocab.rhythm[symbol.rhythm] + position
    39→    return 1000000 + position
    40→
    41→
    42→def _chord_to_str(chord: list[EncodedSymbol]) -> str:
    43→    sorted_chord = sorted(chord, key=_symbol_to_sortable)
    44→    upper_slurs_ties = set()
    45→    lower_slurs_ties = set()
    46→    annotation_resorted: list[EncodedSymbol] = []
    47→    for symbol in sorted_chord:
    48→        stripped, symbol_stripped = symbol.strip_articulations([], remove_all=True)
    49→        for articulation in stripped:
    50→            if symbol.position == "lower":
    51→                lower_slurs_ties.add(articulation)
    52→            else:
    53→                upper_slurs_ties.add(articulation)
    54→        annotation_resorted.append(symbol_stripped)
    55→    if len(upper_slurs_ties) > 0:
    56→        first_upper = next(
    57→            (idx for idx, s in enumerate(annotation_resorted) if s.position != "lower"), None
    58→        )
    59→        if first_upper is not None:
    60→            annotation_resorted[first_upper] = annotation_resorted[first_upper].add_articulations(
    61→                list(upper_slurs_ties)
    62→            )
    63→    if len(lower_slurs_ties) > 0:
    64→        first_lower = next(
    65→            (idx for idx, s in enumerate(annotation_resorted) if s.position == "lower"), None
    66→        )
    67→        if first_lower is not None:
    68→            annotation_resorted[first_lower] = annotation_resorted[first_lower].add_articulations(
    69→                list(lower_slurs_ties)
    70→            )
    71→    return str.join("&", [str(c) for c in annotation_resorted])
    72→
    73→
    74→def calc_ratio_of_tuplets(symbols: list[EncodedSymbol]) -> float:
    75→    tuplets = [s for s in symbols if s.is_tuplet()]
    76→    return float(len(tuplets)) / len(symbols)
    77→
    78→
    79→def token_lines_to_str(symbols: list[EncodedSymbol]) -> str:
    80→    chords = sort_token_chords(symbols)
    81→
    82→    chord_strings = [_chord_to_str(c) for c in chords]
    83→    return str.join("\n", chord_strings)
    84→
    85→
    86→def read_token_lines(lines: list[str]) -> list[EncodedSymbol]:
    87→    result = []
    88→    for line in lines:
    89→        entries = line.split("&")
    90→        for i, entry in enumerate(entries):
    91→            if "tieSlur" in entry:
    92→                continue
    93→            parts = entry.strip().split()
    94→            if len(parts) == 5:
    95→                rhythm, pitch, lift, articulation, position = parts
    96→            else:
    97→                rhythm, pitch, lift, articulation = parts
    98→                position = "upper"
    99→            symbol = EncodedSymbol(rhythm, pitch, lift, articulation, position)
   100→            is_first = i == 0
   101→            if not is_first:
   102→                result.append(EncodedSymbol("chord"))
   103→
   104→            result.append(symbol)
   105→
   106→    return result
   107→
   108→
   109→def read_tokens(filepath: str) -> list[EncodedSymbol]:
   110→    with open(filepath, encoding="utf-8") as f:
   111→        lines = f.readlines()
   112→        return read_token_lines(lines)
   113→
   114→
   115→class DecoderBranches:
   116→    def __init__(
   117→        self,
   118→        rhythms: torch.Tensor,
   119→        pitchs: torch.Tensor,
   120→        lifts: torch.Tensor,
   121→        articulations: torch.Tensor,
   122→        positions: torch.Tensor,
   123→        mask: torch.Tensor,
   124→    ) -> None:
   125→        self.rhythms = rhythms
   126→        self.pitchs = pitchs
   127→        self.lifts = lifts
   128→        self.articulations = articulations
   129→        self.positions = positions
   130→        self.mask = mask
   131→
   132→
   133→def to_decoder_branches(symbols: list[EncodedSymbol]) -> DecoderBranches:
   134→    nonote_token = default_config.nonote_token
   135→    begin_of_seq = vocab.rhythm["BOS"]
   136→    end_of_seq = vocab.rhythm["EOS"]
   137→    rhythms = [begin_of_seq]
   138→    pitchs = [nonote_token]
   139→    lifts = [nonote_token]
   140→    articulations = [nonote_token]
   141→    position = [nonote_token]
   142→    mask = [True]
   143→    for symbol in symbols:
   144→        rhythms.append(vocab.rhythm[symbol.rhythm])
   145→        pitchs.append(vocab.pitch[symbol.pitch])
   146→        lifts.append(vocab.lift[symbol.lift])
   147→        articulations.append(vocab.articulation[symbol.articulation])
   148→        position.append(vocab.position[symbol.position])
   149→        mask.append(True)
   150→
   151→    rhythms.append(end_of_seq)
   152→    pitchs.append(nonote_token)
   153→    lifts.append(nonote_token)
   154→    articulations.append(nonote_token)
   155→    position.append(nonote_token)
   156→    mask.append(True)
   157→
   158→    while len(rhythms) < default_config.max_seq_len:
   159→        rhythms.append(default_config.pad_token)
   160→        pitchs.append(nonote_token)
   161→        lifts.append(nonote_token)
   162→        articulations.append(nonote_token)
   163→        position.append(nonote_token)
   164→        mask.append(False)
   165→
   166→    return DecoderBranches(
   167→        rhythms=torch.tensor(rhythms),
   168→        lifts=torch.tensor(lifts),
   169→        articulations=torch.tensor(articulations),
   170→        pitchs=torch.tensor(pitchs),
   171→        positions=torch.tensor(position),
   172→        mask=torch.tensor(mask),
   173→    )
   174→
   175→
   176→class VocabularyStats:
   177→    def __init__(self) -> None:
   178→        self.rhythm: dict[str, int] = defaultdict(int)
   179→        self.lift: dict[str, int] = defaultdict(int)
   180→        self.articulation: dict[str, int] = defaultdict(int)
   181→        self.pitch: dict[str, int] = defaultdict(int)
   182→        self.max_seq_len = 0
   183→
   184→    def add_lines(self, lines: list[EncodedSymbol]) -> None:
   185→        for line in lines:
   186→            self.rhythm[line.rhythm] += 1
   187→            self.lift[line.lift] += 1
   188→            self.articulation[line.articulation] += 1
   189→            self.pitch[line.pitch] += 1
   190→        self.max_seq_len = max(self.max_seq_len, len(lines))
   191→
   192→    def __str__(self) -> str:
   193→        lines = [
   194→            json.dumps(self.rhythm, indent=2, sort_keys=True),
   195→            json.dumps(self.lift, indent=2, sort_keys=True),
   196→            json.dumps(self.articulation, indent=2, sort_keys=True),
   197→            json.dumps(self.pitch, indent=2, sort_keys=True),
   198→            f"max_seq_len={self.max_seq_len}",
   199→        ]
   200→        return str.join("\n", lines)
   201→
   202→    def __repr__(self) -> str:
   203→        return str(self)
   204→
   205→
   206→if __name__ == "__main__":
   207→    import glob
   208→    import os
   209→    import sys
   210→
   211→    from homr.simple_logging import eprint
   212→
   213→    stats = VocabularyStats()
   214→    errors: set[str] = set()
   215→    i = 0
   216→    if len(sys.argv) > 1:
   217→        files = []
   218→        for index_path in sys.argv[1:]:
   219→            index_file = open(index_path)
   220→            index_lines = index_file.readlines()
   221→            index_file.close()
   222→            files.extend([line.strip().split(",")[1] for line in index_lines])
   223→    else:
   224→        files = glob.glob(os.path.join("datasets", "**", "**.tokens"), recursive=True)
   225→
   226→    for file in files:
   227→        try:
   228→            tokens = read_tokens(file)
   229→            stats.add_lines(tokens)
   230→            check_token_lines(tokens)
   231→            i += 1
   232→            if i % 1000 == 0:
   233→                eprint(i, len(errors))
   234→        except Exception as e:
   235→            eprint("======", file, "======")
   236→            eprint(e)
   237→            errors.add(file)
   238→
   239→    eprint("Stats", stats)
   240→    if len(errors) > 0:
   241→        eprint("errors", errors)
   242→

1→from collections import defaultdict
     2→from typing import Any
     3→
     4→import torch
     5→from torch import Tensor
     6→from transformers import Trainer
     7→
     8→LOSS_COMPONENTS = [
     9→    "loss_rhythm",
    10→    "loss_pitch",
    11→    "loss_lift",
    12→    "loss_consist",
    13→    "loss_position",
    14→    "loss_articulations",
    15→]
    16→
    17→
    18→class HomrTrainer(Trainer):
    19→    """
    20→    Custom Trainer for HOMR transformer.
    21→    Tracks per-branch losses, per-branch token accuracy,
    22→    and overall token-weighted (micro) accuracy.
    23→    """
    24→
    25→    def __init__(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
    26→        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
    27→
    28→        # Training loss accumulators
    29→        self._loss_sum: dict[str, float] = defaultdict(float)
    30→        self._loss_count: dict[str, int] = defaultdict(int)
    31→
    32→        # Evaluation accumulators
    33→        self._eval_loss_sum: dict[str, float] = defaultdict(float)
    34→        self._eval_loss_count: dict[str, int] = defaultdict(int)
    35→        self._acc_correct: dict[str, int] = defaultdict(int)
    36→        self._acc_total: dict[str, int] = defaultdict(int)
    37→
    38→    def compute_loss(
    39→        self,
    40→        model: torch.nn.Module,
    41→        inputs: dict[str, Any],
    42→        return_outputs: bool = False,
    43→        num_items_in_batch: Tensor | int | None = None,
    44→    ) -> torch.Tensor | tuple[torch.Tensor, dict[str, torch.Tensor]]:
    45→        if model.training:
    46→            # Calculate sampling probability
    47→            config = getattr(model, "config", None)
    48→            if config:
    49→                start_p = config.scheduled_sampling_start_prob
    50→                end_p = config.scheduled_sampling_end_prob
    51→                decay = config.scheduled_sampling_decay_steps
    52→                step = self.state.global_step
    53→                sampling_prob = max(end_p, start_p - (start_p - end_p) * (step / decay))
    54→                inputs["sampling_prob"] = sampling_prob
    55→            else:
    56→                inputs["sampling_prob"] = 1.0
    57→
    58→        outputs = model(**inputs)
    59→        loss = outputs["loss"]
    60→
    61→        if model.training:
    62→            for component in LOSS_COMPONENTS:
    63→                self._loss_sum[component] += outputs[component].detach().item()
    64→                self._loss_count[component] += 1
    65→
    66→        if return_outputs:
    67→            return loss, outputs
    68→        return loss
    69→
    70→    def prediction_step(
    71→        self,
    72→        model: torch.nn.Module,
    73→        inputs: dict[str, torch.Tensor],
    74→        prediction_loss_only: bool,
    75→        ignore_keys: list[str] | None = None,
    76→    ) -> tuple[torch.Tensor | None, torch.Tensor | None, torch.Tensor | None]:
    77→
    78→        inputs = self._prepare_inputs(inputs)
    79→
    80→        with torch.no_grad():
    81→            outputs = model(**inputs)
    82→
    83→        # Accumulate per-branch eval losses
    84→        for k in LOSS_COMPONENTS:
    85→            self._eval_loss_sum[k] += outputs[k].detach().item()
    86→            self._eval_loss_count[k] += 1
    87→
    88→        # Accumulate per-branch accuracy stats
    89→        self._accumulate_accuracy(outputs, inputs)
    90→
    91→        loss = outputs["loss"].mean().detach().cpu()
    92→        return loss, None, None
    93→
    94→    def _accumulate_accuracy(
    95→        self,
    96→        outputs: dict[str, Any],
    97→        inputs: dict[str, torch.Tensor],
    98→    ) -> None:
    99→        rhythm, pitch, lift, position, articulations = outputs["logits"]
   100→        # Pull binary mask from inputs and align with y_out (shift by 1)
   101→        eval_mask = inputs["mask"][:, 1:]
   102→
   103→        branches = [
   104→            ("rhythm", rhythm, inputs["rhythms"]),
   105→            ("pitch", pitch, inputs["pitchs"]),
   106→            ("lift", lift, inputs["lifts"]),
   107→            ("position", position, inputs["positions"]),
   108→            ("articulations", articulations, inputs["articulations"]),
   109→        ]
   110→
   111→        for name, logits, input_labels in branches:
   112→            preds = logits.argmax(dim=-1)
   113→            labels = input_labels[:, 1:]  # next-token alignment
   114→
   115→            min_len = min(preds.shape[1], labels.shape[1], eval_mask.shape[1])
   116→            preds = preds[:, :min_len]
   117→            labels = labels[:, :min_len]
   118→            current_eval_mask = eval_mask[:, :min_len]
   119→
   120→            # Combine explicit ignore_index with the binary mask
   121→            mask = (labels != -100) & current_eval_mask
   122→            if not mask.any():
   123→                continue
   124→
   125→            correct = ((preds == labels) & mask).sum().item()
   126→            total = mask.sum().item()
   127→
   128→            self._acc_correct[name] += int(correct)
   129→            self._acc_total[name] += int(total)
   130→
   131→    def evaluation_loop(self, *args, metric_key_prefix="eval", **kwargs):  # type: ignore
   132→        # Reset eval accumulators
   133→        self._eval_loss_sum.clear()
   134→        self._eval_loss_count.clear()
   135→        self._acc_correct.clear()
   136→        self._acc_total.clear()
   137→
   138→        output = super().evaluation_loop(
   139→            *args,
   140→            metric_key_prefix=metric_key_prefix,
   141→            **kwargs,
   142→        )
   143→
   144→        # Per-component eval losses
   145→        for k in LOSS_COMPONENTS:
   146→            if self._eval_loss_count[k] > 0:
   147→                output.metrics[f"{metric_key_prefix}_{k}"] = (
   148→                    self._eval_loss_sum[k] / self._eval_loss_count[k]
   149→                )
   150→
   151→        # Per-branch accuracies
   152→        for k in self._acc_total:
   153→            if self._acc_total[k] > 0:
   154→                output.metrics[f"{metric_key_prefix}_{k}_accuracy"] = (
   155→                    self._acc_correct[k] / self._acc_total[k]
   156→                )
   157→
   158→        # Overall token-weighted (micro) accuracy
   159→        total_correct = sum(self._acc_correct.values())
   160→        total_tokens = sum(self._acc_total.values())

file.content:
from collections import defaultdict
from typing import Any

import torch
from torch import Tensor
from transformers import Trainer

LOSS_COMPONENTS = [
    "loss_rhythm",
    "loss_pitch",
    "loss_lift",
    "loss_consist",
    "loss_position",
    "loss_articulations",
]


class HomrTrainer(Trainer):
    """
    Custom Trainer for HOMR transformer.
    Tracks per-branch losses, per-branch token accuracy,
    and overall token-weighted (micro) accuracy.
    """

    def __init__(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)

        # Training loss accumulators
        self._loss_sum: dict[str, float] = defaultdict(float)
        self._loss_count: dict[str, int] = defaultdict(int)

        # Evaluation accumulators
        self._eval_loss_sum: dict[str, float] = defaultdict(float)
        self._eval_loss_count: dict[str, int] = defaultdict(int)
        self._acc_correct: dict[str, int] = defaultdict(int)
        self._acc_total: dict[str, int] = defaultdict(int)

    def compute_loss(
        self,
        model: torch.nn.Module,
        inputs: dict[str, Any],
        return_outputs: bool = False,
        num_items_in_batch: Tensor | int | None = None,
    ) -> torch.Tensor | tuple[torch.Tensor, dict[str, torch.Tensor]]:
        if model.training:
            # Calculate sampling probability
            config = getattr(model, "config", None)
            if config:
                start_p = config.scheduled_sampling_start_prob
                end_p = config.scheduled_sampling_end_prob
                decay = config.scheduled_sampling_decay_steps
                step = self.state.global_step
                sampling_prob = max(end_p, start_p - (start_p - end_p) * (step / decay))
                inputs["sampling_prob"] = sampling_prob
            else:
                inputs["sampling_prob"] = 1.0

        outputs = model(**inputs)
        loss = outputs["loss"]

        if model.training:
            for component in LOSS_COMPONENTS:
                self._loss_sum[component] += outputs[component].detach().item()
                self._loss_count[component] += 1

        if return_outputs:
            return loss, outputs
        return loss

    def prediction_step(
        self,
        model: torch.nn.Module,
        inputs: dict[str, torch.Tensor],
        prediction_loss_only: bool,
        ignore_keys: list[str] | None = None,
    ) -> tuple[torch.Tensor | None, torch.Tensor | None, torch.Tensor | None]:

        inputs = self._prepare_inputs(inputs)

        with torch.no_grad():
            outputs = model(**inputs)

        # Accumulate per-branch eval losses
        for k in LOSS_COMPONENTS:
            self._eval_loss_sum[k] += outputs[k].detach().item()
            self._eval_loss_count[k] += 1

        # Accumulate per-branch accuracy stats
        self._accumulate_accuracy(outputs, inputs)

        loss = outputs["loss"].mean().detach().cpu()
        return loss, None, None

    def _accumulate_accuracy(
        self,
        outputs: dict[str, Any],
        inputs: dict[str, torch.Tensor],
    ) -> None:
        rhythm, pitch, lift, position, articulations = outputs["logits"]
        # Pull binary mask from inputs and align with y_out (shift by 1)
        eval_mask = inputs["mask"][:, 1:]

        branches = [
            ("rhythm", rhythm, inputs["rhythms"]),
            ("pitch", pitch, inputs["pitchs"]),
            ("lift", lift, inputs["lifts"]),
            ("position", position, inputs["positions"]),
            ("articulations", articulations, inputs["articulations"]),
        ]

        for name, logits, input_labels in branches:
            preds = logits.argmax(dim=-1)
            labels = input_labels[:, 1:]  # next-token alignment

            min_len = min(preds.shape[1], labels.shape[1], eval_mask.shape[1])
            preds = preds[:, :min_len]
            labels = labels[:, :min_len]
            current_eval_mask = eval_mask[:, :min_len]

            # Combine explicit ignore_index with the binary mask
            mask = (labels != -100) & current_eval_mask
            if not mask.any():
                continue

            correct = ((preds == labels) & mask).sum().item()
            total = mask.sum().item()

            self._acc_correct[name] += int(correct)
            self._acc_total[name] += int(total)

    def evaluation_loop(self, *args, metric_key_prefix="eval", **kwargs):  # type: ignore
        # Reset eval accumulators
        self._eval_loss_sum.clear()
        self._eval_loss_count.clear()
        self._acc_correct.clear()
        self._acc_total.clear()

        output = super().evaluation_loop(
            *args,
            metric_key_prefix=metric_key_prefix,
            **kwargs,
        )

        # Per-component eval losses
        for k in LOSS_COMPONENTS:
            if self._eval_loss_count[k] > 0:
                output.metrics[f"{metric_key_prefix}_{k}"] = (
                    self._eval_loss_sum[k] / self._eval_loss_count[k]
                )

        # Per-branch accuracies
        for k in self._acc_total:
            if self._acc_total[k] > 0:
                output.metrics[f"{metric_key_prefix}_{k}_accuracy"] = (
                    self._acc_correct[k] / self._acc_total[k]
                )

        # Overall token-weighted (micro) accuracy
        total_correct = sum(self._acc_correct.values())
        total_tokens = sum(self._acc_total.values())
        if total_tokens > 0:
            output.metrics[f"{metric_key_prefix}_accuracy"] = total_correct / total_tokens

        return output

    def log(self, logs: dict[str, float], start_time: float | None = None) -> None:
        if self._loss_count and "loss" in logs:
            for component in LOSS_COMPONENTS:
                if self._loss_count[component] > 0:
                    logs[component] = self._loss_sum[component] / self._loss_count[component]
            self._loss_sum.clear()
            self._loss_count.clear()

        super().log(logs, start_time)

content.text:
Here's what the training system does:

## Training Overview

It trains the **transformer model** that converts dewarped staff images into tokenized music sequences. This is the core recognition model — the thing that looks at a single staff line image and outputs notes.

### Architecture

**Encoder-decoder transformer** (`TrOMR`):
- **Encoder**: Takes a staff image (fixed canvas size) and produces context embeddings
- **Decoder**: 5 parallel heads that decode simultaneously from the shared encoder context:
  - `rhythm` — note duration (quarter, eighth, rest, chord, barline, etc.)
  - `pitch` — note pitch (C4, D#5, etc.)
  - `lift` — whether the note lifts off the staff
  - `articulation` — slurs, ties, etc.
  - `position` — upper/lower staff position (for grand staff)

### Datasets

Three sources, mixed together with equal weights:
1. **PrIMuS** — printed music dataset (single-staff lines)
2. **Lieder** — art songs (grand staff, piano+voice)
3. **GrandStaff** — piano grand staff dataset

Each sample is a pair: `(staff_image.png, tokens.txt)` where the tokens file contains the ground truth sequence in a custom 5-branch format.

### Training Details

- **Framework**: HuggingFace `Trainer` (via custom `HomrTrainer`)
- **Loss**: Sum of per-branch cross-entropy losses + a consistency loss
- **Optimizer**: AdamW fused, cosine LR schedule, 0.1 warmup
- **Batch size**: 18 (bf16) or 6 (fp32), with 4x gradient accumulation
- **Epochs**: 35 (full), 15 (fine-tune), 10 (smoke test)
- **Backbone freezing**: Encoder backbone frozen for first 2 epochs, then unfrozen
- **Scheduled sampling**: Starts teacher-forced, gradually shifts to autoregressive
- **Early stopping**: Patience of 5 epochs on `eval_accuracy`
- **Data augmentation**: Image distortion + random occlusions during training
- **Metric**: Token-weighted micro accuracy across all 5 branches
- **Logging**: TensorBoard

### Fine-tuning Mode

There's a `--fine` mode that:
- Loads from a checkpoint
- Freezes encoder + decoder, unfreezes only the **lift decoder** head
- Lower learning rate (1e-5 vs 1e-4)
- Fewer epochs (15)

### Output

Saves a `.pth` file to `training/architecture/transformer/pytorch_model_{run_id}.pth`, which then needs to be converted to ONNX via `training/onnx/` for inference.

It's working correctly. 5 pieces done so far (piece-006 is in progress), each with:

- **Per-piece `diagnosis.json`** with SER, error counts, per-page/per-measure breakdowns
- **Per-page `.musicxml` outputs** copied into the run directory
- Effort correctly detected as `historical-eval-20260314` from the branch name
- Commit `29e394f` matches current HEAD

The `.musicxml` and `.npy` files are also being written into `corpus/` (by homr's `--cache` flag) — that's expected since homr writes output next to the input image. Those are the cache files and outputs that will persist for future runs.

One thing to note: piece-006 has no diagnosis yet (still processing), and pieces vary significantly — piece-004 is at 2% SER (great) while piece-001 is at 46% (rough). This spread is normal and useful for identifying where the model struggles.

The run looks healthy. It'll finish all 69 pieces, finalize the directory name with the average SER, generate HTML reports, and update PROGRESS.md.

# Training log

This log contains the major milestones of the training process, as well as failed attempts to improve training. The focus of the training of the transformer and the segmentation model is documented only briefly.

## How to train

### Segmentation model (segnet)

`training/train.py segnet`

This will download the dataset and start the training. Training takes about 1 hour.

### Transformer (TrOmr)

Prerequisites:

- Linux
- CUDA
- rsvg-convert, e.g. via `sudo apt install librsvg2-bin`

Download the datasets and convert them to the format required for training:

- `training/datasets/convert_primus.py`
- `training/datasets/convert_grandstaff.py`
- `training/datasets/convert_lieder.py`  
  This will also download and run MuseScore as an AppImage. If this fails, check your setup to ensure that you can run `datasets/MuseScore`.

Some checks:

- `training/transformer/training_vocabulary.py`: Check that the vocabulary is complete
- `training/validate_music_xml_conversion.py`: Can be used to test changes in MusicXML parsing and generation
- `training/validate_music_xml_conversion.py`: Visualize datasets; takes one of `datasets/*/index.txt` as an argument

Finally, start the training itself with: `training/train.py transformer`.  
This takes around 2–4 days.

## Results

The `homr` pipeline is a two-step system consisting of **staff detection (segnet)** followed by **music transcription (TrOmr transformer)**.  
Accordingly, we report results from two different validation setups with different purposes and scopes.

### Transcription Smoke Test

The _Transcription Smoke Test_ evaluates only the transformer-based transcription component on a small, fixed dataset.  
It is intended as a **fast indicator** of transcription model quality and is mainly used to detect regressions or larger performance changes during development.

Implementation: `symbol_error_rate_torch.py`

### System Level Validation

The _System Level Validation_ evaluates the full `homr` pipeline, including staff detection and transcription, on a dedicated test dataset.  
This validation provides a **more representative indication of overall system performance** and is used to compare training runs and pipeline changes.

**Note:** The test dataset cannot be published due to copyright restrictions. In addition, the dataset is subject to change over time, which may affect the comparability of results across different runs.

Implementation: `rate_validation_result.py`

file.content:
import json
import os
from typing import Any

from homr.transformer.vocabulary import Vocabulary

workspace = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__))
root_dir = os.getcwd()


class FilePaths:
    def __init__(self) -> None:
        model_name = "pytorch_model_331-e10346542968cc71fbcce0c0696f3ac963f11ae1"
        self.encoder_path = os.path.join(
            workspace,
            f"encoder_{model_name}.onnx",
        )  # noqa: E501
        self.decoder_path = os.path.join(
            workspace,
            f"decoder_{model_name}.onnx",
        )  # noqa: E501

        self.encoder_path_fp16 = os.path.join(
            workspace,
            f"encoder_{model_name}_fp16.onnx",
        )  # noqa: E501
        self.decoder_path_fp16 = os.path.join(
            workspace,
            f"decoder_{model_name}_fp16.onnx",
        )  # noqa: E501

        self.checkpoint = os.path.join(
            root_dir,
            "training",
            "architecture",
            "transformer",
            f"{model_name}.pth",
        )

        self.rhythmtokenizer = os.path.join(workspace, "tokenizer_rhythm.json")
        self.lifttokenizer = os.path.join(workspace, "tokenizer_lift.json")
        self.pitchtokenizer = os.path.join(workspace, "tokenizer_pitch.json")
        self.notetokenizer = os.path.join(workspace, "tokenizer_note.json")

    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
        return {
            "checkpoint": self.checkpoint,
            "rhythmtokenizer": self.rhythmtokenizer,
            "lifttokenizer": self.lifttokenizer,
            "pitchtokenizer": self.pitchtokenizer,
            "notetokenizer": self.notetokenizer,
        }

    def to_json_string(self) -> str:
        return json.dumps(self.to_dict(), indent=2)


class DecoderArgs:
    def __init__(self) -> None:
        self.attn_on_attn = True
        self.cross_attend = True
        self.ff_glu = True
        self.rel_pos_bias = False
        self.use_scalenorm = False
        self.attn_dropout = 0.1
        self.ff_dropout = 0.1
        self.layer_dropout = 0.1

    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
        return {
            "attn_on_attn": self.attn_on_attn,
            "cross_attend": self.cross_attend,
            "ff_glu": self.ff_glu,
            "rel_pos_bias": self.rel_pos_bias,
            "use_scalenorm": self.use_scalenorm,
            "attn_dropout": self.attn_dropout,
            "ff_dropout": self.ff_dropout,
            "layer_dropout": self.layer_dropout,
        }

    def to_json_string(self) -> str:
        return json.dumps(self.to_dict(), indent=2)


class Config:
    def __init__(self) -> None:
        self.vocab = Vocabulary()
        self.filepaths = FilePaths()
        self.channels = 1
        self.patch_size = 16
        self.max_height = 256
        self.max_width = 1280
        self.max_seq_len = 608
        self.pad_token = 0
        self.bos_token = 1
        self.eos_token = 2
        self.nonote_token = 0
        self.num_rhythm_tokens = len(self.vocab.rhythm)
        self.num_pitch_tokens = len(self.vocab.pitch)
        self.num_lift_tokens = len(self.vocab.lift)
        self.num_articulation_tokens = len(self.vocab.articulation)
        self.num_position_tokens = len(self.vocab.position)
        self.encoder_structure = "convnext"
        self.encoder_depth = 8
        self.backbone_layers = [3, 4, 6, 3]
        self.encoder_dim = 512
        # encoder_h_dim balances how many dimensions the
        # horizontal vs vertical embeddings get
        self.encoder_h_dim = self.encoder_dim // 3
        self.encoder_heads = 8
        self.decoder_dim = self.encoder_dim
        self.decoder_depth = 8
        self.decoder_heads = 8
        self.decoder_args = DecoderArgs()
        self.lift_vocab = self.vocab.lift
        self.pitch_vocab = self.vocab.pitch
        self.rhythm_vocab = self.vocab.rhythm
        self.articulation_vocab = self.vocab.articulation
        self.position_vocab = self.vocab.position
        self.use_gpu_inference = True
        self.enable_geometric_correction = (
            os.environ.get("HOMR_DISABLE_GEOMETRIC_CORRECTION") != "1"
        )

        # Scheduled Sampling parameters
        self.scheduled_sampling_start_prob = 1.0
        self.scheduled_sampling_end_prob = 0.7
        self.scheduled_sampling_decay_steps = 20000

    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
        return {
            "filepaths": self.filepaths.to_dict(),
            "channels": self.channels,
            "patch_size": self.patch_size,
            "max_height": self.max_height,
            "max_width": self.max_width,
            "max_seq_len": self.max_seq_len,
            "pad_token": self.pad_token,
            "bos_token": self.bos_token,
            "eos_token": self.eos_token,
            "nonote_token": self.nonote_token,
            "encoder_structure": self.encoder_structure,
            "encoder_depth": self.encoder_depth,
            "backbone_layers": self.backbone_layers,
            "encoder_dim": self.encoder_dim,
            "encoder_heads": self.encoder_heads,
            "num_rhythm_tokens": self.num_rhythm_tokens,
            "decoder_dim": self.decoder_dim,
            "decoder_depth": self.decoder_depth,
            "decoder_heads": self.decoder_heads,
            "decoder_args": self.decoder_args.to_dict(),
        }

    def to_json_string(self) -> str:
        return json.dumps(self.to_dict(), indent=2)


# Initialize the Config class
default_config = Config()

## What You're Running vs What Exists

**Currently deployed model**: Run 331 (`configs.py` line 13)
- `pytorch_model_331-e10346542968cc71fbcce0c0696f3ac963f11ae1`
- Smoke Test SER: **6%**
- System Level: **5.3 diffs, SER 3.4%**
- Architecture: ConvNext encoder + scheduled sampling

**Best model that exists**: Also Run 331. It's the best performing model in the training history.

**Run 334** (the most recent training run) actually regressed:
- Smoke Test SER: 6% (same)
- System Level: **10.2 diffs, SER 13.6%** (much worse)
- Used `convnextv2_base` — bigger backbone didn't help at system level

## So There's No Better Model Sitting Around

Run 331 is already the best trained model. The gap between its **3.4% SER on the upstream test set** and the **~30-46% SER you're seeing on your corpus** tells you something important:

**The bottleneck isn't the transformer model itself — it's everything upstream of it:**

1. **Staff detection** — if staves are detected wrong, the transformer gets garbage input
2. **Dewarping** — perspective correction affects what the transformer sees
3. **Segmentation** — if noteheads/stems aren't classified correctly, anchors fail
4. **Your corpus is harder** — camera photos of real sheet music vs the cleaner test set upstream uses

Training a better transformer model would help if the transformer were the bottleneck. But with 3.4% SER on clean input, it's already quite good. The errors you're seeing are more likely from the pipeline stages *before* the transformer gets involved.

That said, there are training-side things that *could* help:
- **Fine-tuning on your specific corpus** — if your sheet music has characteristics not well-represented in PrIMuS/Lieder/GrandStaff
- **Training with more aggressive dewarping augmentation** — to make the transformer more robust to the imperfect dewarping it gets from the pipeline
- **A better segmentation model** — the segnet was only trained once and briefly mentioned

But first, the historical eval data you're generating right now will tell you *where* the errors actually come from, which is the right starting point.

message.content: Are you speaking of training in general or whats in the ./training folder?

content.text:
Good challenge. I was speaking in general — let me be more precise about what's actually in `./training/`.

The `./training/` folder trains **the models that ship with homr**. Specifically:

1. **`training/train.py transformer`** — trains the TrOMR transformer (encoder-decoder that reads staff images → token sequences). This produces a `.pth` file that gets converted to ONNX via `training/onnx/`

2. **`training/train.py segnet`** — trains the UNet segmentation model (classifies each pixel as background/stem/notehead/clef/staff/symbol)

That's it. Everything else in the pipeline — staff detection, anchor reconstruction, dewarping, post-processing, MusicXML generation — is **handwritten code with no trained components**. Those stages are pure algorithmic logic in `staff_detection.py`, `bounding_boxes.py`, `staff_parsing.py`, `music_xml_generator.py`, etc.

So to directly answer your question: `./training/` can only improve two things:
- The transformer's ability to read a clean staff image
- The segmentation model's ability to classify pixels

It **cannot** improve staff detection, dewarping, symbol grouping, or MusicXML generation — those would require code changes, not retraining.

Whether the errors you're seeing come from the trained models or the algorithmic code is exactly what the historical eval should help answer. Once the run finishes, we can look at the error categories to see where the damage is.

This is the exact problem the old `run_historical.sh` hit too. Commit `4eaaaca` (post-merge) has an older version of `brace_dot_detection.py` that doesn't have `group_staves_by_spacing` — but `poetry install` installed the *current* version of the `homr` package from the worktree's code.

The issue: `poetry lock --no-update` regenerated the lock file, which changed the worktree's files. And the `homr` package itself is installed as an editable/local package, so if the lock resolved against code that references a function that doesn't exist at that commit, it breaks.

Let me check what's happening.

content.text:
Here's the full audit. I'll categorize everything:

## 1. EVAL CODE — move to homr-eval or delete from repo

Already copied to homr-eval. **Delete from repo:**

| Path | Verdict |
|------|---------|
| `evaluation/` (entire directory) | **Delete** — scripts already in homr-eval, sources are 1.4M lines of XML bloating the repo |
| `run_historical.sh` | **Delete** — replaced by worktree workflow |
| `compare_historical.py` | **Delete** — replaced by `homr-eval/scripts/compare_runs.py` |
| `validation/diagnose_musicxml.py` | **Delete** — copied to homr-eval |
| `validation/diagnose_errors.py` | **Delete** — eval support |
| `validation/diagnose_confidence.py` | **Delete** — eval support |
| `validation/diagnose_page_warp.py` | **Delete** — eval support |
| `validation/run_diagnosis.py` | **Delete** — eval support |
| `validation/run_capture.py` | **Delete** — eval support |
| `validation/run_comparison.sh` | **Delete** — eval support |
| `validation/replay_correction.py` | **Delete** — eval support |
| `validation/experiment_geometric_correction.py` | **Delete** — eval support |
| `validation/compare_musicxml.py` | **Delete** — eval support |
| `test_roundtrip.sh` | **Delete** — eval script |
| `test_roundtrip_full.py` | **Delete** — eval script |
| `test_geometric_pitch.py` | **Delete** — experiment script |
| `plans/*.md` | **Delete** — planning docs, not code |

## 2. REAL BUG FIXES — keep, PR to main

These are actual code fixes in `homr/`:

| Commit | File(s) | What |
|--------|---------|------|
| `f414858` | `homr/main.py` | Fix broken import from merge |
| `7966930` | `homr/main.py` | Remove unused cv2 import from merge |
| `e9b3724` | `homr/staff_detection.py` | Staff detection improvements |
| `08715e0` | `homr/staff_detection.py` | Rescue line matching when subset check fails |
| `aba9466` | `homr/staff_detection.py` | Merge colinear connected lines |
| `ad13d94` | `homr/staff_detection.py` | Prevent cross-staff merging |
| `aba27b7` | various | Remove incompatible correction capture |

## 3. NEW FEATURES — decide whether to keep

| File(s) | What | Verdict |
|---------|------|---------|
| `homr/geometric_pitch_correction.py` | Geometric pitch correction module | **Keep if it helps accuracy**, but it's wired in behind an env var toggle |
| `homr/pipeline_trace.py` | Debug tracing | **Keep** — useful diagnostics |
| `homr/find_peaks.py` | Peak finding for staff detection | **Keep** — used by staff_detection changes |
| `homr/staff_dewarping.py` changes | Major dewarping improvements | **Keep if they help** |
| `tests/test_geometric_pitch_correction.py` | Unit tests | **Keep** if keeping the feature |
| `tests/test_clef_classification.py` | New tests | **Keep** |
| `tests/test_model.py` | New tests | **Keep** |
| `tests/test_page_warp_consensus.py` | New tests | **Keep** |
| `tests/test_spatial_grouping.py` | New tests | **Keep** |

## 4. UPSTREAM CHANGES (came with the merge)

Everything below `4eaaaca` in the log is upstream — already on main. Not your concern.

## Recommended Action

1. **Delete all eval/validation/planning files** from the repo — they're in homr-eval now
2. **Cherry-pick the bug fixes** (items in section 2) onto a clean branch from main
3. **Decide on geometric pitch correction** — the eval results will tell you if it helps
4. **PR the fixes** to main as a clean branch

Want me to start cleaning up the repo?

message.content: yes

content.text: Now also remove `CLAUDE.md` (that's our project-specific instructions, not something for upstream) and the Makefile eval targets:

content.content:
diff --git a/Makefile b/Makefile
index 1068c84..b2573d4 100644
--- a/Makefile
+++ b/Makefile
@@ -77,6 +77,44 @@ run-pre-commit:
 pre-commit: set-pre-commit run-pre-commit
 
 
+# Evaluation
+eval-render:
+	@poetry run python -m evaluation.setup_corpus --render
+
+eval-setup:
+	@poetry run python -m evaluation.setup_corpus
+
+eval:
+	@poetry run python -m evaluation.run_evaluation --from-existing
+
+eval-run:
+	@poetry run python -m evaluation.run_evaluation
+
+eval-piece:
+	@test -n "$(PIECE)" || (echo "Usage: make eval-piece PIECE=piece-004" && exit 1)
+	@poetry run python -m evaluation.run_evaluation --piece $(PIECE)
+
+eval-piece-debug:
+	@test -n "$(PIECE)" || (echo "Usage: make eval-piece-debug PIECE=piece-004" && exit 1)
+	@poetry run python -m evaluation.run_evaluation --piece $(PIECE) --debug
+
+eval-compare:
+	@poetry run python -m evaluation.compare_runs
+
+eval-compare-piece:
+	@test -n "$(PIECE)" || (echo "Usage: make eval-compare-piece PIECE=piece-005 [RUN_A=... RUN_B=...]" && exit 1)
+	@poetry run python -m evaluation.compare_runs $(RUN_A) $(RUN_B) --piece $(PIECE)
+
+eval-diagnose:
+	@poetry run python -m validation.run_diagnosis
+
+eval-html:
+	@poetry run python -m evaluation.generate_html
+
+eval-open:
+	@xdg-open evaluation/runs/index.html 2>/dev/null || open evaluation/runs/index.html 2>/dev/null || echo "Open evaluation/runs/index.html in your browser"
+
+
 # Documentation
 update-doc:
 	@poetry run sphinx-apidoc --module-first --no-toc -o docs/source libs

+	@test -n "$(PIECE)" || (echo "Usage: make eval-piece PIECE=piece-004" && exit 1)
+	@poetry run python -m evaluation.run_evaluation --piece $(PIECE)
+
+eval-piece-debug:
+	@test -n "$(PIECE)" || (echo "Usage: make eval-piece-debug PIECE=piece-004" && exit 1)
+	@poetry run python -m evaluation.run_evaluation --piece $(PIECE) --debug
+
+eval-compare:
+	@poetry run python -m evaluation.compare_runs
+
+eval-compare-piece:
+	@test -n "$(PIECE)" || (echo "Usage: make eval-compare-piece PIECE=piece-005 [RUN_A=... RUN_B=...]" && exit 1)
+	@poetry run python -m evaluation.compare_runs $(RUN_A) $(RUN_B) --piece $(PIECE)
+
+eval-diagnose:
+	@poetry run python -m validation.run_diagnosis
+
+eval-html:
+	@poetry run python -m evaluation.generate_html
+
+eval-open:
+	@xdg-open evaluation/runs/index.html 2>/dev/null || open evaluation/runs/index.html 2>/dev/null || echo "Open evaluation/runs/index.html in your browser"
+
+
 # Documentation
 update-doc:
 	@poetry run sphinx-apidoc --module-first --no-toc -o docs/source libs
